message:
test(record): debug_assert slot.header_offset is inside the template

encode_record_with_header_template writes serial_byte into buf at
slot.header_offset when the slot needs a runtime patch. The
header_offset comes from the RecordHeaderTemplate so it should
always be within header_size, but the code was relying on that
being correct without asserting it.

Added a `debug_assert!(slot.header_offset < header_size);` immediately
before the write. If the header construction ever drifts such that
a patch slot points past the header_size, this fires in debug builds
and on the test suite with a clear message instead of silently
scribbling into the body region (where the subsequent
append_serialized_value would then overwrite it anyway, producing a
completely confusing test failure downstream).

Zero cost in release builds.
